Houston blasts GLO over plans to strip $52M in Harvey funding

The Texas General Land Office plans to take control of $52 million of Hurricane Harvey disaster funds currently controlled by the City of Houston and intended for public service and economic development programs and move the money into a state-controlled homeowner assistance program.

On June 11, George and Kathleen Rowe were sued by their homeowners association for feeding ducks. The requested relief? Up to a quarter of a million dollars and a court order never to do it again.

Houston urologists say vasectomies on the rise after Roe v. Wade

The demand for vasectomies has surged in some parts of the country after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade, and some Houston urologists are seeing the same trend locally as Texas prepares to ban almost all abortions with few exceptions.
